betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Landing Float§f is a §6single block§f landing gear for AR rockets that land on §6liquid surfaces§f — water, oil, or any §6AR-compatible fluid§f. It replaces the standard landing legs when the destination is a gas giant or an ocean world, providing buoyancy and preventing the rocket from sinking on touchdown.

§6Production Path (libVulpes RecipesMachine)§f:
- Built via §6Crafting Table§f: registered through `RecipesMachine.getInstance()` for the landing float class
- The AR source does not expose a direct JSON recipe; the recipe is added at runtime by the libVulpes plate presser system
- The float is a §6single block§f that occupies one cell of the rocket frame's bottom layer

The landing float has §6zero use_count§f in the JEI graph (use_count: 0 in the evidence), meaning it is consumed only when placed in a rocket and not used as an ingredient in any other recipe. It is functionally a §6buoyancy block§f that generates an upward force equal to the gravity of the planet the rocket is landing on.

The landing float is a prerequisite for any §6ocean world§f or §6gas giant cloud city§f mission. It is also required for §6Earth-like worlds§f with > 50% ocean coverage because the rocket would otherwise splashdown and damage its frame.

§eTip§f: Place the landing float at the §6center bottom block§f of the rocket frame. Multiple floats can be stacked on a wide rocket (5×5 or larger) for extra buoyancy on gas giants where the atmosphere is dense enough to require positive lift.
